steps.title: Identify what the verb must agree with
steps.content: The subject of this clause is “The chapter”—a singular noun. The relative clause “whose boundaries encompass 83,400 acres” is a modifier that doesn't change the subject's number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
The rule of subject-verb agreement requires the verb to match the subject in number.

A singular subject requires a singular verb. “Is” is the only singular present-tense form and correctly agrees with “The chapter.” The other choices are plural forms (A, B) or past-tense (C), violating subject-verb agreement.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
